summary: Iranian negotiators advanced talks in Switzerland, including a de‑confliction cell for Lebanon, while Israel reported the killing of a Nukhba terrorist linked to the Gaza hostage case. Hezbollah faced a deep Israeli strike 12 km inside Lebanon as Israeli leadership reaffirmed commitment to southern Lebanon security. Regional actors, including Qatar and Oman, engaged in diplomatic moves concerning the Strait of Hormuz and broader war‑ending discussions.
key_points: Iranian officials met in Switzerland and agreed to establish a de‑confliction cell to monitor cease‑fire compliance in Lebanon (Source: @almogboker78/17560).
Israel’s IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Nukhba operative Sabaa’i Zahir Abd al‑Hamed Abu Hasna, involved in the Oct 7 attack and hostage‑holding (Source: @almogboker78/17568).
A deep Israeli operation reached about 12 km inside Lebanon targeting Hezbollah positions (Source: @idf_telegram/24418).
Iranian parliament speaker Qalibaf travelled to Muscat to discuss Strait of Hormuz cooperation with Oman's Sultan (Source: @VahidOnline/76598).
Iran denied U.S. claims that IAEA inspectors were allowed into Iran, stating no approval was given (Source: @asafroz/50622).
Qatar publicly backed Doha’s role in Iran‑U.S. negotiations in Switzerland (Source: @SharghDaily/167572).
Hezbollah’s influence was highlighted as a factor Iran must control to preserve Lebanese stability (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74211).
Israeli senior leaders met to coordinate continued operations against threats in southern Lebanon (Source: @almogboker78/17571).

sections.executive_summary: Iranian negotiators in Switzerland agreed to a de‑confliction cell for Lebanon, while Israel killed a Gaza‑linked Nukhba terrorist. A deep Israeli strike 12 km inside Lebanon targeted Hezbollah, and Israeli leadership reaffirmed its southern Lebanon security posture. Regional diplomacy continued with Qatar’s support and Iran’s outreach to Oman on the Strait of Hormuz.

points: Iranian officials in Switzerland concluded an agreement to set up a de‑confliction cell aimed at monitoring cease‑fire compliance in Lebanon, though no direct reference to Hezbollah was made (Source: @almogboker78/17560).

Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad‑Bagher Qalibaf flew from Switzerland to Muscat to meet Sultan Haitham, discussing cooperation on the Strait of Hormuz and bilateral ties (Source: @VahidOnline/76598).

Iran’s foreign ministry spokesman Ismail Baghaei reported progress in Swiss talks, citing discussions on ending the war on all fronts, including Lebanon, and on oil sales and the Hormuz shipping corridor (Source: @VahidOnline/76584).
Iran denied U.S. statements that IAEA inspectors had been granted entry, stating no official approval was issued by Iranian negotiating teams (Source: @asafroz/50622).
Qatar’s foreign minister emphasized Doha’s full support for the Iran‑U.S. negotiations taking place in Switzerland (Source: @SharghDaily/167572).

The IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Nukhba operative Sabaa’i Zahir Abd al‑Hamed Abu Hasna, who had entered Israel during the Oct 7 attacks and participated in the kidnapping of Omer Shammam held by Hamas (Source: @almogboker78/17568).

Israeli senior leadership—including the Prime Minister, Defense Minister, Chief of Staff, and Northern Command commander—held a meeting reaffirming relentless action to neutralize threats to soldiers and civilians and to maintain the security zone in southern Lebanon (Source: @almogboker78/17571).
A reported Israeli operation penetrated approximately 12 km inside Lebanese territory, representing the deepest strike against Hezbollah forces to date (Source: @idf_telegram/24418).
Israeli officials emphasized continued destruction of terrorist infrastructure and protection of the civilian population along the northern border (Source: @almogboker78/17571).
Israeli security forces announced the release of reserve units in the north in preparation for a cease‑fire, indicating a shift in force posture (Source: @lelotsenzura/93160).
Israeli security forces reported the killing of a Nukhba terrorist from Gaza who had taken part in the Oct 7 massacre and in the kidnapping of a hostage held by Hamas (Source: @almogboker78/17568).
Hezbollah’s role was highlighted as a stability factor, with Iranian officials stating that Iran must control Hezbollah to preserve Lebanese security (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74211).
Israel conducted its deepest incursion yet, reaching about 12 km inside Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah positions as part of a broader operation (Source: @idf_telegram/24418).
Israeli senior commanders discussed ongoing operations to suppress threats and maintain the security zone along the southern Lebanese border (Source: @almogboker78/17571).
Iranian parliament speaker Qalibaf met with Oman’s Sultan in Muscat to discuss cooperation on the Strait of Hormuz and broader bilateral issues (Source: @VahidOnline/76598).
Qatar’s foreign minister publicly backed Doha’s involvement in the Iran‑U.S. talks held in Switzerland (Source: @SharghDaily/167572).

key_developments: Iranian negotiators in Switzerland agreed to create a de‑confliction cell to monitor cease‑fire compliance in Lebanon, signaling a diplomatic mechanism to reduce cross‑border incidents (Source: @almogboker78/17560).

Israel’s IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Nukhba operative Sabaa’i Zahir Abd al‑Hamed Abu Hasna, linked to the Oct 7 attacks and hostage‑taking, reducing immediate terrorist threat (Source: @almogboker78/17568).
A deep Israeli strike reached roughly 12 km inside Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah infrastructure and marking the most extensive cross‑border operation to date (Source: @idf_telegram/24418).
Iran denied that IAEA inspectors had been authorized to enter Iran, maintaining its stance against external nuclear oversight (Source: @asafroz/50622).
Iranian parliament speaker Qalibaf’s visit to Muscat focused on Strait of Hormuz cooperation, indicating Iran’s outreach to Gulf states amid sanctions (Source: @VahidOnline/76598).
Qatar’s foreign minister affirmed Doha’s full support for the Iran‑U.S. negotiations in Switzerland, highlighting regional diplomatic backing (Source: @SharghDaily/167572).
Iranian officials stated that controlling Hezbollah is essential for Lebanese stability, underscoring Tehran’s influence over Lebanese militia dynamics (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74211).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah remains a primary conventional threat to Israel, evidenced by deep Israeli incursions inside Lebanese territory. Iran’s diplomatic activity, including the de‑confliction cell and outreach to Oman, suggests an intent to manage regional escalation while maintaining leverage over proxy forces. The elimination of a Nukhba operative reduces immediate Gaza‑linked terrorist capability but does not alter the broader insurgent threat landscape.
sections.regional_response: Qatar’s explicit support for the Iran‑U.S. talks reflects a regional desire to stabilize the Strait of Hormuz and mitigate broader conflict spillover. Oman’s engagement with Iranian leadership indicates a willingness to act as a diplomatic conduit. Israel’s reinforced security posture in the north and south demonstrates a commitment to countering Hezbollah and Gaza‑linked threats, while maintaining pressure on adversaries.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring the implementation of the de‑confliction cell will be critical to assess compliance and prevent inadvertent clashes along the Lebanon‑Israel border. Continued surveillance of Hezbollah’s operational capacity is needed following the deep Israeli strike. Iranian diplomatic moves in the Gulf, particularly concerning Hormuz, warrant close observation for potential shifts in maritime security dynamics.
